div1.entryFree:
Violently, 1) vehemently, forcibly, impetuously: which being v. borne upon, Err. I, 1, 103. thou art v. carried away from grace, H4A II, 4, 491. temperately proceed to what you would thus v. redress, Cor. III, 1, 220. discharged of breath as v. as hasty powder fired doth hurry, Rom. V, 1, 64. if you v. proceed against him, Lr. I, 2, 89.
2) by force: v. ravished her, Lucr. Arg. Lr. I, 2, 89
